slides.title: Motilal Oswal Financial Services Initiation Note
slides.subtitle: Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd – BUY
slides.rationale: Introduce report with rating, market price and target

slides.title: Investment Thesis
slides.rationale: Summarise core re‑rating theme and supporting evidence

blocks.content: We initiate a BUY view based on a decisive shift from cyclical broking revenue to an annuity‑driven earnings mix. The growing share of recurring revenue reduces volatility and aligns the franchise with higher‑multiple investors. Recent credit upgrade underscores balance‑sheet strength, while fee‑based businesses demonstrate robust scaling.

items: Recurring revenue mix deepens, lowering earnings cyclicality
Fee‑based assets under management expand on a compounding trajectory
Operating leverage improves as fixed cost base supports margin growth
AA+ credit rating validates resilient capital structure
Probability‑weighted valuation captures upside across regulatory scenarios

slides.title: Company Snapshot
slides.rationale: Provide at‑a‑glance overview and key metrics

blocks.content: Motilal Oswal Financial Services delivers integrated capital‑markets, wealth, housing finance and alternative‑investment solutions across India. Revenue is generated from brokerage, investment banking, asset‑management fees and wealth‑management advisory, with an increasing tilt toward fee‑based income.

India’s financial‑services sector acts as a shock absorber for broader economic growth. The wealth market is expanding rapidly, with high‑net‑worth households expected to surge, creating a sizable addressable base for asset‑management and private‑wealth services. Mutual‑fund penetration remains low, indicating ample room for fee‑based growth, while NBFCs are scaling profitably.

items: Wealth‑creation trends boost demand for asset‑management and advisory services
Low mutual‑fund penetration signals latent fee‑based revenue opportunity
NBFC profitability rising on credit‑market expansion
Regulatory clarity on derivatives trading expected to stabilise broking margins
Digital adoption accelerates distribution efficiency across franchise

slides.title: Key Highlights
slides.rationale: Anchor thematic pillars for the investment case

items: ARR Share: Recurring revenue now dominates the earnings mix
AUM Growth: Asset base expanding at a compounding rate
Credit Quality: AA+ rating reinforces balance‑sheet resilience
Valuation Gap: Market price offers material discount to consensus target
Governance: Monitoring required for ongoing SEBI matters
SAARTHI Score: Strong 72/100 reflecting robust business fundamentals

slides.title: Revenue & EBITDA Mix by Segment
slides.rationale: Show revenue contribution and profitability across business lines

blocks.content: The franchise is diversifying, with asset‑management and private‑wealth segments contributing an increasing share of revenue and EBITDA, while traditional broking remains a smaller, more cyclical component.

slides.title: DCF Summary
slides.rationale: Display core DCF assumptions and key output

blocks.content: The discounted cash‑flow model incorporates a weighted‑average cost of capital reflective of the company's capital structure and growth profile. Terminal growth assumptions align with long‑term GDP expansion, and cash‑flow forecasts integrate the shifting revenue mix.

slides.title: SAARTHI Score 72/100
slides.rationale: Present proprietary quality score and underlying dimensions

slides.title: Forensic – Monitor Closely
slides.rationale: Highlight governance and regulatory matters requiring watch

slides.title: Trading Strategy
slides.rationale: Outline entry, review and exit considerations

blocks.content: Given the valuation discount and upside potential, a strategic entry near current levels is warranted. Review earnings trajectory and regulatory updates quarterly. Consider partial profit‑taking as price approaches consensus target, maintaining exposure for the long‑run fee‑based earnings upside.

items: Enter near current market price to capture valuation gap
Reassess after each quarterly earnings release
Scale out incrementally as price nears consensus target
Maintain exposure for fee‑based earnings upside beyond target
Regulatory changes to F&O trading could compress broking margins
Quarterly earnings volatility may intensify if cyclical revenue falls
Competitive pressure from discount brokers and fintech platforms
Ongoing SEBI matters require close oversight
Mark‑to‑market fluctuations on proprietary book could impact earnings
Housing‑finance asset quality could deteriorate in stressed credit cycles
